prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_case student_enrollment_1)
(register_case module_enrollment_1)
(register_case administrative_case_record_1)
(register_case administrative_case_record_2)
(assign_case_handler student_enrollment_1 case_handler_1)
(assign_case_handler module_enrollment_1 case_handler_2)
(attach_medical_document student_enrollment_1 medical_document_1)
(validate_medical_evidence student_enrollment_1 medical_document_1)
(submit_supporting_evidence_student student_enrollment_1 slot_a_1 supporting_evidence_1)
(progress_student_negotiation student_enrollment_1 slot_a_1 medical_document_1 supporting_evidence_1)
(unlink_medical_document student_enrollment_1 medical_document_1)
(attach_medical_document module_enrollment_1 medical_document_1)
(validate_medical_evidence module_enrollment_1 medical_document_1)
(submit_supporting_evidence_module module_enrollment_1 slot_b_1 supporting_evidence_1)
(progress_module_negotiation module_enrollment_1 slot_b_1 medical_document_1 supporting_evidence_1)
(unlink_medical_document module_enrollment_1 medical_document_1)
(synthesize_proposal_standard student_enrollment_1 module_enrollment_1 slot_a_1 slot_b_1 replan_proposal_1)
(attach_medical_document student_enrollment_1 medical_document_1)
(finalize_proposal replan_proposal_1 student_enrollment_1 medical_document_1)
(mark_student_enrollment_ready_for_certificate_binding student_enrollment_1 replan_proposal_1)
(mark_module_enrollment_ready_for_certificate_binding module_enrollment_1 replan_proposal_1)
(bind_medical_certificate_type student_enrollment_1 medical_certificate_type_1 medical_document_1)
(apply_replan_to_student_enrollment student_enrollment_1 case_handler_1 medical_certificate_type_1)
(unlink_medical_document student_enrollment_1 medical_document_1)
(attach_medical_document module_enrollment_1 medical_document_1)
(bind_medical_certificate_type module_enrollment_1 medical_certificate_type_1 medical_document_1)
(apply_replan_to_module_enrollment module_enrollment_1 case_handler_2 medical_certificate_type_1)
(unlink_medical_document module_enrollment_1 medical_document_1)
(assign_case_handler administrative_case_record_1 case_handler_1)
(attach_medical_document administrative_case_record_1 medical_document_1)
(validate_medical_evidence administrative_case_record_1 medical_document_1)
(assign_availability_token_to_case administrative_case_record_1 availability_token_1)
(link_external_authority_reference administrative_case_record_1 external_authority_1)
(assign_faculty_to_case administrative_case_record_1 faculty_representative_1)
(attach_approval_document_to_proposal administrative_case_record_1 approval_document_1 replan_proposal_1)
(run_compliance_stage_one_check administrative_case_record_1 approval_document_1 replan_proposal_1 medical_document_1)
(run_compliance_stage_two administrative_case_record_1 availability_token_1 faculty_representative_1 approval_document_1 replan_proposal_1)
(perform_external_authority_review_basic administrative_case_record_1 external_authority_1 approval_document_1 replan_proposal_1)
(mark_case_ready_for_certificate_binding administrative_case_record_1)
(bind_medical_certificate_type administrative_case_record_1 medical_certificate_type_1 medical_document_1)
(apply_replan_to_case_record administrative_case_record_1 case_handler_1 medical_certificate_type_1)
(unlink_medical_document administrative_case_record_1 medical_document_1)
(release_availability_token_from_case administrative_case_record_1 availability_token_1)
(unassign_faculty_from_case administrative_case_record_1 faculty_representative_1)
(unlink_external_authority_reference administrative_case_record_1 external_authority_1)
(assign_case_handler administrative_case_record_2 case_handler_2)
(attach_medical_document administrative_case_record_2 medical_document_1)
(validate_medical_evidence administrative_case_record_2 medical_document_1)
(assign_availability_token_to_case administrative_case_record_2 availability_token_1)
(link_external_authority_reference administrative_case_record_2 external_authority_1)
(assign_faculty_to_case administrative_case_record_2 faculty_representative_1)
(attach_approval_document_to_proposal administrative_case_record_2 approval_document_2 replan_proposal_1)
(run_compliance_stage_one_check administrative_case_record_2 approval_document_2 replan_proposal_1 medical_document_1)
(run_compliance_stage_two administrative_case_record_2 availability_token_1 faculty_representative_1 approval_document_2 replan_proposal_1)
(perform_external_authority_review_basic administrative_case_record_2 external_authority_1 approval_document_2 replan_proposal_1)
(mark_case_ready_for_certificate_binding administrative_case_record_2)
(bind_medical_certificate_type administrative_case_record_2 medical_certificate_type_1 medical_document_1)
(apply_replan_to_case_record administrative_case_record_2 case_handler_2 medical_certificate_type_1)
```

The plan achieves the goal by systematically processing each case subject (student_enrollment_1, module_enrollment_1, administrative_case_record_1, and administrative_case_record
message.reasoning:
The problem involves a health-related absence course replanning domain. The goal is to apply replan to student_enrollment_1, module_enrollment_1, administrative_case_record_1, and administrative_case_record_2.


## Investigating replanning process  
- The task focuses on replanning within a health-related absence course domain.  
- It involves applying replan to specific entities: student_enrollment_1, module_enrollment_1, and two administrative_case_records.

## Investigating replanning process  
- Medical documents, faculty representatives, and supporting evidence are available for the process.  
- Slots are reserved for student_enrollment_1 and module_enrollment_1, with replan_proposal_1 and approval documents linked to cases.  

- Policy references, attachments, and external authority are accessible, supporting case management.  
- Approval tokens and medical certificate type are available, ensuring compliance for certificate binding.  

## Checking case registration  
- No cases are registered yet; need to register student_enrollment_1, module_enrollment_1, and two administrative_case_records.  
- All are classified as case_subjects, allowing registration with prerequisites like not being registered or replan_applied.

## Investigating replanning process  
- To apply replan, each case needs medical_certificate_attached, case_assigned_to a handler, and case_bound_to_medical_certificate_type.  
- Apply_replan sets replan_applied, makes the handler and certificate type available, and requires prior binding steps.  

- Cases can reach ready_for_certificate_binding via compliance paths like mark_case_ready_for_certificate_binding or finalize_case_compliance.  
- For student and module enrollments, a negotiation path with supporting evidence can also achieve ready_for_certificate_binding.  

## Checking case readiness  
- Student enrollment requires registration, handler assignment, and medical document attachment before validation.  
- Negotiation for students involves submitting and progressing evidence, confirming slots without needing faculty interaction.  

- Module enrollment follows a similar evidence-based negotiation path, reusing available supporting evidence.  
- Both paths ensure cases are validated and ready for certificate binding, supporting parallel processing where possible.

## Examining compliance stages  
- Compliance checks involve stage1 and stage2, with stage2 requiring faculty assignment and an available token.  
- Stage2 effects include passing compliance, but it needs prior validation and faculty linked to the case.  

## Analyzing resource allocation  
- Resources like faculty, availability tokens, and medical documents are shared across cases, requiring careful sequencing.  
- Faculty assignment is necessary for admin cases during stage2, but can be unassigned later to free up for other tasks.  

## Outlining process flow  
- Negotiations for student and module enrollments must happen first, followed by proposal synthesis and finalization.  
- Admin cases require compliance checks post-negotiation, with faculty involvement, before reaching ready_for_certificate_binding.

## Extending process flow  
- All four cases (student, module, and two admin) require validation, necessitating handler assignment, as handlers remain assigned until the end.  
- Initially, only three handlers are available, suggesting a need to sequence case processing to reuse handlers after releasing them.  

## Planning handler reuse  
- After applying replan to student and module cases, their handlers can be released and reassigned to admin cases.  
- Register all four cases first, then assign handlers to student and module, process them to completion, and reuse handlers for admin cases.

## Continuing process flow  
- Student and module negotiations require medical document attachment for validation, with the document reusable after unlinking.  
- After confirming student negotiation, the document remains attached, requiring unlinking before attaching to the module for its negotiation.  

## Managing document attachment  
- Document must be attached to the student for proposal finalization, but after module negotiation, it's attached to the module.  
- Unlinking the document from the module and reattaching it to the student is necessary before finalizing the proposal.

## Managing document attachment  
- After applying replan to student enrollment, the medical document remains attached to the student, requiring unlinking to make it available for module binding.  
- For module binding, the document must be attached to the module first, then unbound after replan to free it for admin case processing.  

## Assigning case handlers  
- Handlers become available after replan is applied to student and module cases, allowing reassignment to admin cases.  
- Admin cases are assigned available handlers, with one handler left unused, optimizing resource reuse.  

## Processing admin cases  
- Admin cases require the medical document to be attached and validated before compliance checks, following document unlinking from the module.  
- Compliance for admin cases involves attaching an approval document to the finalized proposal, consuming it and linking it to the proposal for stage1 checks.

## Sequencing handler assignments  
- After student and module cases are replanned, handlers 1 and 2 become available for reassignment to admin cases, with handler 3 left unused.  
- Admin cases can be processed sequentially, assigning handler 1 to admin1 and handler 2 to admin2, optimizing resource use.  

## Managing document availability  
- Medical document must be unlinked from the module after replan, then attached to admin1 for validation before processing admin2.  
- Only one availability token exists, requiring admin cases to be processed one after the other to ensure token availability.  

## Handling external resources  
- External authority and faculty are assigned to cases for stage2, consumed during review, then unlinked for reuse in subsequent cases like admin2.  
- Policy attachments and validations are available but not used in this process, focusing on medical document and compliance checks.  

## Finalizing proposal flow  
- After finalizing the proposal, the document remains attached to the student, requiring unlinking after binding and applying student replan.  
- The document then cycles to module and admin cases, ensuring each step unlinks and reattaches for the next process.

## Registering and Processing Cases  
- Two administrative cases, administrative_case_record_1 and administrative_case_record_2, are registered for processing.  
- Student and module cases are assigned handlers, with case_handler_1 for student_enrollment_1 and case_handler_2 for module_enrollment_1, preparing for further steps.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
